raw_text.response: Yeah. The problem states that we should discard two diagonally opposed corners of 8x8 checkerboard. If I understand correctly, these two diagonally opposed colors actually have a same color. If it's black, the other one is black. If it's white, the other one is white. And if we cover the checkerboard with dominoes, we always have to cover adjacent tiles. And in checkerboard, they have different colors. So eventually, if we use 31 diamonds to cover all 62 squares, we should cover 31 black cells and 31 white cells. However, because we eliminated two cells of the same color from the checkerboard, then we will not eventually be able to cover all 62 squares with 31 diamonds in our hands.
